subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The exploration follows a recognisable flow (introduction, method, calculations, limitations, conclusion), aiding reader navigation.
The title is concise and clearly identifies the focus on Taiwanâs area modelling.
Headings, numbered figures, and appendices provide a basic structure allowing material to be located.
weaknesses: Frequent digressions (full tables, repeated formula explanations) interrupt the argument and reduce coherence.
Sections feel disjointed (e.g. late appearance of scale conversion), weakening logical development.
Large unfiltered data tables add redundancy and interrupt the flow.
Inconsistent section numbering and inconsistent referencing of appendices disrupt structure.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work achieves moderate coherence, with an adequate but not sophisticated organizational structure. Content is mostly relevant but marred by repetition and unnecessary detail. These factors align with a Band 2 performance, reflecting adequate presentation without the clarity or conciseness expected at higher bands.

strengths: Standard mathematical symbols (â«, dx, | |) and function notation are used throughout.
Multiple representations (graphs, tables, algebraic expressions) demonstrate more than a minimal range.
Calculation steps are presented in execution order, providing some logical clarity.
weaknesses: Notational inconsistencies (omitted dx, lost superscripts, mixed units) reduce precision.
Graphs are low resolution, axes are unlabeled, and screenshots hinder interpretability.
Many equations and outputs lack context or explanation, and tables often omit units and headers.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission shows some consistent use of mathematical language and multiple representations, but lapses in notation, labeling, and clarity limit communication to Band 2. The logical structure exists but is not sufficiently clear or consistently supported by accurate notation.

strengths: The rationale shows genuine personal interest in geography and climate change, tying the topic to the studentâs background.
The decision to trace Taiwanâs outline and split it into zones demonstrates some initiative.
The conclusion connects personal interest with knowledge gained, indicating reflective thought.
weaknesses: Most methods (FitPoly, integration) follow standard procedures with limited deviation.
Independent thinking is present but restricted; few novel modelling choices are made.
Creativity is modest, and once errors appear, little inventive adjustment is implemented.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of personal engagement is clear but limited in depth and innovation. The student shows some independence and personal approach, meeting Band 1 criteria but not achieving the higher engagement levels of Band 2 or 3.

strengths: The student identifies and discusses human error, rounding, and scale factor uncertainties.
Basic analysis includes percentage differences and acknowledgement of result inaccuracies.
Limitations section lists potential errors and proposes generic improvements.
weaknesses: Reflections are brief and mostly descriptive, lacking probing questions or alternative strategies.
No systematic analysis of model underestimation or polynomial fit effects.
Evaluation of outcomes does not quantify uncertainties or propose detailed future work.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but remains superficial and descriptive. Critical analysis and evaluation of outcomes occur only at a basic level, consistent with Band 1 rather than the deeper insight of higher bands.

strengths: Polynomial fitting, definite integration, and scale conversion are all pertinent and appropriately chosen for area estimation.
Mathematical techniques used (piecewise functions, integration) are commensurate with the SL syllabus.
The traced map and domain-restricted plots correctly apply piecewise definitions, showing solid methodological understanding.
weaknesses: Several expressions are copied inaccurately; unit conversions are flawed and inconsistently applied.
Scale-conversion formula contains errors, and its placement late in the work disconnects it from modelling steps.
Integral formulas occasionally omit function definitions or correct notation, undermining accuracy.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics employed is clearly relevant and at the appropriate SL level, demonstrating a sound selection of techniques (Band 3). Execution errors and unit inconsistencies prevent a higher score, but the studentâs thorough engagement with courseâlevel mathematics warrants a Band 3 allocation.

strandSelections.reasoning: Steps in the calculation are presented in the order carried out, but explanations are sometimes ambiguous (e.g. rescaling procedure), and several equations are copied without context.  This provides some clarity but does not reach the level of âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ.

The student demonstrates the technique of integrating polynomials, but several expressions are copied inaccurately, units are confused, and the final numerical result is far from accepted values.  This indicates only some understanding with noticeable errors rather than good or thorough mastery.

The student employs tables of coordinates, multiple graphs and algebraic expressions, demonstrating more than limited representation.  However, many graphs are screenshots with low resolution and axes unlabeled, and there is little linkage between the different forms, so the representations are helpful but not fully effective.

The exploration follows a recognisable flow â introduction, method, calculations, limitations and conclusion â so the reader can generally see how the work progresses.  Nevertheless, the argument is frequently interrupted by long digressions (tables copied in full, screen-shots, repeated explanations of the same formula) and several sections feel disjointed (e.g. the scale conversion appears long after the integration).  The logical link between the modelling choices and the stated aim is only partially maintained, so overall coherence is moderate rather than strong.

Using GeoGebra and splitting the shape into 16 zones displays a modest initiative, yet the modelling itself is conventional and there is little inventive adjustment once errors are revealed.  Creativity and initiative are therefore present but limited.

Some basic analysis is attempted (percentage difference, acknowledgement of rounding errors), yet there is no systematic investigation of why the model underestimates area or how polynomial fitting affects accuracy.  The analysis therefore remains basic.

The student notes the sizeable discrepancy with the published area and lists potential improvements, but stops short of quantitatively evaluating uncertainty or proposing concrete future work.  This satisfies only a limited evaluation of outcomes.

The student remarks on accuracy issues, human error and scale factors, but these reflections are brief and mostly descriptive.  They do not probe underlying causes or alternative strategies in depth, so the reflection is considered limited rather than meaningful.

Standard symbols (â«, dx, | |) and function notation are used, yet inconsistently: several integrals omit dx, superscripts are lost, units are mixed (cm, m and km), and variable names change (h, h1, h1x).  These lapses show some but not consistent control of mathematical language.

A short rationale links the topic to the studentâs interest in geography, but the subsequent work largely mirrors standard cartographic-integration tasks.  Hence only some personal approach can be identified.

Most sections relate to the stated aim, but much text is repetitive (the integration formula is explained three times) and large tables of raw GeoGebra output add little value.  While the student does attempt to keep irrelevant narrative to a minimum, noticeable redundancy prevents the exploration from being described as concise.

Polynomial fitting, definite integration and scale conversion are all pertinent to estimating an irregular area, so the mathematics chosen is clearly relevant.  Although the execution is imperfect, the selection itself is appropriate for the problem.

The choice to trace Taiwan and devise a multi-function approach shows the student made some decisions, yet most techniques (FitPoly, domain restriction, basic definite integrals) follow textbook or teacher-led methods.  Independent thinking is evident but limited.

Headings, numbered figures and appendices give the work a basic structure and allow the reader to locate material.  However, figures are sometimes out of order, some captions are misleading and Appendix material is not always referenced.  Because of these organisational weaknesses the structure is judged adequate rather than clear or sophisticated.

The work employs integration, piece-wise functions and scale factors, which are solidly within the AA-SL syllabus and used at a level consistent with that course.  There is no significant use of mathematics above or below the required level.

annotations.comment: The introduction provides useful climate change context but lacks a precise mathematical aim statement; consider explicitly stating the modelling objective and expected outcomes.
The title is concise and clearly identifies the focus of the exploration on Taiwanâs area modeling, effectively orienting the reader.
The raw GeoGebra map screenshot is low resolution and axes are unlabeled, reducing clarity and making precise interpretation difficult.
The summary of Taiwan landmass change uses percentage but skips the calculation steps; include brief workings to enhance transparency.
The explanation of scale conversion is embedded long after modelling steps and feels disconnected; integrate scale methodology earlier and with clear formulaic support.
The rationale demonstrates genuine personal interest in geography and climate change, showing clear engagement with the chosen topic.
The output polynomial is copied without superscripts and omits context; ensure each term is written as $$-0.0000127x^5+0.0020429x^4-0.1276263x^3+\dots$$.
The plotted functions figure lacks axis labels and scale annotations; add labels (x, y axes, units) to improve interpretability.
The traced map figure shows initiative in digital tracing, providing a reproducible outline for further analysis.
The large unfiltered coordinate table adds redundancy and interrupts flow; summarize key columns in the main text and relegate full data to an appendix.
The table lacks units on axes and column headers, hindering mathematical clarity; include units (cm, m) consistently.
The description of FitPoly use explains the tool but variable naming is unclear; define each function symbol and its domain in a formal list.
The domain-restriction formula lacks clarity in notation; consider standard notation $$h_i(x)=	ext{If}(x_{start}\\le x \\le x_{end}, f_i(x))$$ for each segment.
The piecewise function input is formatted inconsistently across lines, making it hard to follow; keep each definition on one line and use clear function names.
The text indicates 18 sections but earlier refers to 16; this inconsistency disrupts coherenceâreconcile section counts throughout.
The domain-restricted plot captures the outline well, demonstrating correct application of piecewise functions.
The fully carved perimeter figure effectively illustrates the final traced shape, aiding subsequent area integration.
The caption for Figure 8 is vague; specify which functions or segments are shown and how they relate to the perimeter.

The integral formula is missing the function in the first integrand; it should read $$
\int_a^b [g(x)-f(x)]\,dx$$ with both functions clearly stated.
The CAS code output mixes typographical errors ("0.110.711...") and dimension inconsistencies; clean up syntax and denote units uniformly.
The negative result in Area 13 is reported then converted via absolute value but lacks critical discussion; reflect on why the integral is negative.
The detailed reflection on human error, rounding and scale factors shows thoughtful awareness of uncertainties in modelling.
The description of integration steps for Area 1 is unclear; label each equation numerically and reference them in your explanation.
The scale-conversion formula squares both area and length inconsistently, yielding flawed units; re-derive using $$ igl(	frac{scale	imes length_{map}}{length_{map}} igr)^2$$.
The limitations section lists possible errors but remains descriptive; deepen critical reflection by quantifying potential impacts of each source of error.
Table 2âs caption implies comprehensive calculation but the table format is cluttered; reformat rows and columns for readability.
The conclusion effectively connects personal interest, knowledge gained and potential extensions, demonstrating reflective depth.
The bibliography entries vary in style and completeness; use a consistent citation format (APA/MLA) and ensure each entry has author, date, and source.
The appendices contain extensive equations but are never referenced in the main text; add cross-references to guide the reader.
Appendix B clearly shows piecewise definitions but would be more effective if key segments were summarized in the text.
